nodes.x: Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,

“ Wo boliovc that all governments necessarily require civil officers and magistrates to enforce tho same ; and that such as will administer the law in equity and justice should be sought for, and uphold by tho voice of the poople (if a republic,) or the will of tho sovereign.

We believe that all governments necessarily require civil officers and magistrates to enforce the laws of the same; and that such as will administer the law in equity and justice should be sought for and upheld by the voice of the people if a republic, or the will of the sovereign.
To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.

But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil.
Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
For your obedience is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you wise unto that which is good, and simple concerning evil.
For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:

We who were once slaves of lust, now have delight only in purity
    of morals; we, who once practiced arts of magic, have consecrated
    ourselves to the Eternal and Good God; we, who once prized gain
    above all things, give even what we have to the common use, and
    share it with such as are in need; we, who once hated and murdered
    one another, who on account of differences of customs…
Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts of our flesh, fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind; and were by nature the children of wrath, even as others.
But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,
And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.
And God saw that the wickedness of men had become great in the earth; and every man was lifted up in the imagination of the thoughts of his heart, being only evil continually.
For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the Lord endureth for ever. Praise ye the Lord.
And we saw a vision of the sufferings of those with whom he made war and overcame, for thus came the voice of the Lord unto us:
Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;

like effect in another passage: "But after that the kindness and love of God 
our Savior toward man appeared, not by works of righteousness which we 
have done, but according to His mercy He saved us, by the washing of 
regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost; which He shed on us 
abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior; that, being justified by His 
grace, we should be made heirs…
That through his atonement, and by obedience to the principles of the gospel, mankind might be saved.

And that he manifesteth himself unto all those who believe in him, by the power of the Holy Ghost; yea, unto every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, working mighty miracles, signs, and wonders, among the children of men according to their faith.
Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
Whoso sheddeth man’s blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.
And he that keepeth his commandments dwelleth in him, and he in him. And hereby we know that he abideth in us, by the Spirit which he hath given us.

Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ: according as he hath chosen * us-' in him; before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him inlove: having predestinated' us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will, to the praise of the glory of his…
And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.
But whoso keepeth his word, in him verily is the love of God perfected: hereby know we that we are in him.
That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

position of mankind, under different circumstances, in this state of mortality; but they cannot influence or change the Gospel of the Son of God, or the eternal truths of heaven; they remain unchangeable; as it is said very properly by the Church of England, in one of their homilies, “as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, worlds without end.” If nothing else they say is true,…
But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.

This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto…
For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.

And wo be unto the children of men if this be the case; for there shall be none that doeth good among you, no not one. For if there be one among you that doeth good, he shall work by the power and gifts of God.
Then they say in their hearts: This is not the work of the Lord, for his promises are not fulfilled. But wo unto such, for their reward lurketh beneath, and not from above.
But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
But if it be a question of words and names, and of your law, look ye to it; for I will be no judge of such matters.
I will keep thy statutes: O forsake me not utterly.
But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.
A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;

1 Hom. in Princip, Act. p. 54. ‘* Kirst we must see who 
wrote the Book. . . . whether a man, or God : and if man, let 
us reject it; for, “Call no man master upon earth: but if 
God, let us receive it.”
A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself; but the simple pass on, and are punished.
Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.
He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;
For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.

against him, and he should be frowned upon by all high-minded and virtuous persons. Wealth, influence and position should not screen him from their righteous indignation. His sin is one of the blackest in the calendar of crime, and he should be cast down from the high pinnacle of respectability and consideration, to find his place among the worst of felons. Every virtuous woman desires a husband…
For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, until his iniquity be found to be hateful.
And he that looketh upon a woman to lust after her shall deny the faith, and shall not have the Spirit; and if he repents not he shall be cast out.
When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.
¶ This is the copy of the letter that they sent unto him, even unto Artaxerxes the king; Thy servants the men on this side the river, and at such a time.

¶ And it shall be when the Lord shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he sware unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.
And I will send an angel before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:
Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.
And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.
Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.

get their blessings and prepare them for the future glory of Zion. Let us take that course which will make us independent of all other people upon the earth; I know that this is the course for us to take all the time. Then we should put our minds together, and our mites also, to build up the kingdom of God; and if we will do this, being of one spirit, we shall prosper in all things. I know of no…
Nevertheless, through you shall the oracles be given to another, yea, even unto the church.
And now continue your journey. Assemble yourselves upon the land of Zion; and hold a meeting and rejoice together, and offer a sacrament unto the Most High.
And let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.
Teach them to never be weary of good works, but to be meek and lowly in heart; for such shall find rest to their souls.
For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.
Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.
All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.
All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.
Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is meet, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth;
